title: Closing Richmond's Civic UX Gap: Faster Service Discovery and Smarter Contract Review
summary: Executive overview of the two core City Hall problems: residents cannot reliably navigate rva.gov and RVA311 to find services, and city staff must manually review contracts across four disparate platforms. Establishes key facts on system architecture, data constraints, and top hackathon priorities.

title: Route Right, Buy Smart: A weekend playbook for RVA311 navigation and procurement risk
summary: Comprehensive research framework covering both City Hall problem domains: resident service navigation and procurement contract review. Defines data sources, scope constraints, and weekend-viable build paths.

title: Build-Ready Plan for a Thriving City Hall: Data, Dependencies, and Guardrails for Richmond's Service Navigation
summary: Top 10 research questions that teams must answer before building: covers 311 API availability, rva.gov structure, contract data quality, and champion identification. Includes actionable answers and open gaps.

title: De-risking Richmond's 311 and Procurement MVP: Evidence and Actions
summary: Ten testable hypotheses for both MVP tracks with evidence status and recommended validation actions. Surfaces which assumptions are confirmed, which are inferred, and which require hands-on testing.

title: Red Flags for A Thriving City Hall — Preventing Harmful AI Pitfalls in RVA311 Prototypes
summary: Warning signs that a City Hall prototype is heading toward harm: AI hallucination of service routing, legal claims, data staleness, over-reliance on unavailable 311 API, and misleading civic language.

title: Building Around Blind Spots — Practical Workarounds for a Thriving Richmond City Hall Hackathon
summary: Identifies critical unknowns in the City Hall data landscape and offers practical workarounds for building without them. Covers 311 routing logic, procurement PDF availability, and rva.gov structure gaps.

title: Breaking the Maze: Where Richmond Residents Get Lost in City Services
summary: Detailed problem analysis of resident service navigation failures: department-centric rva.gov information architecture, 311 call volume patterns, category taxonomy fragmentation, and comparable city findings. Identifies where navigation breaks and how it generates phone calls.

title: Richmond's Contract Review Reality: Where Work Breaks and How to Fix It
summary: Root cause analysis of procurement contract review failures: multi-platform fragmentation across City/State/Federal systems, PDF-buried contract terms, and manual cross-system comparison burden on staff.

title: Prototype where the data lives: Richmond 311 vs. Procurement
summary: Side-by-side comparison of the two targeted problem statements on data readiness, feasibility, and risk profile. Helps teams choose between service navigation and procurement review as their hackathon focus.

title: Breaking the Silo: Low-Risk Paths to a Citizen-Centric Richmond
summary: Structural root causes of City Hall UX failures: siloed departmental systems, no unified resident front door, and procurement workflows that assume manual verification. Identifies lowest-risk intervention points.

title: Fixing Findability: Turning RVA.gov's Search and Navigation into a Resident-Centered Service Finder
summary: Deep analysis of rva.gov's search and navigation architecture, including Drupal Search API with Facets, department slug structure, and Google Translate coverage. Proposes task-centric restructuring patterns.

title: Richmond Residents, Real Needs: Designing Plain-Language Service Navigation That Works
summary: Resident user persona analysis covering service-seeking behaviors, vocabulary mismatches with city department naming, phone-first tendencies, and plain-language design principles for digital service navigation.

title: RVA311 routing reality: cutting misroutes, boosting first-time right
summary: 311 call center staff persona covering routing decision logic, common misroute patterns, high-volume request categories, and the gap between digital intake and internal dispatch processes.

title: Closing Richmond's Contract Risk Gap: From Manual Review to Data-Driven Procurement Control
summary: Procurement staff persona covering current manual contract review workflows, multi-platform navigation burden, contract expiry tracking gaps, and the types of automated support that would reduce staff workload.

title: Thriving City Hall: Who Matters, What They Need, and How to Win Support
summary: Stakeholder map for both City Hall problem domains: residents, 311 staff, procurement staff, department heads, IT, and named champions. Includes adoption strategy considerations for each group.

title: Designing Equitable 311 Access for Richmond's Mobile, Multilingual, and Low-Literacy Residents
summary: Digital equity analysis for Richmond's service navigation tools: mobile-first access patterns, multilingual support via Google Translate, low-bandwidth considerations, and inclusive design requirements.

title: RVA311 — From AvePoint/Dynamics Backbone to Actionable, Open-by-Default City Service Data
summary: Complete landscape of the RVA311 system: AvePoint Citizen Services on Dynamics 365, ~70 request categories, integration with Trimble Cityworks via BizTalk, lack of public API, and historical SeeClickFix data access.

title: RVA.gov Search and Structure Playbook: From Department Pages to Citizen Tasks
summary: Detailed inventory of rva.gov structure: 40+ department slugs, Drupal Search API, Facets module, Google Translate widget, sitemap availability, and content organization patterns. Includes how to crawl and use for prototype content.

title: Stitching Richmond's Contract Intelligence: A City-State-Federal Integration Playbook
summary: Inventory of all procurement systems accessible to Richmond teams: City Contracts Socrata (xqn7-jvv2), eVA state system, SAM.gov federal API, and VITA statewide contracts. Covers access modes, limitations, and integration approaches.

title: Bridging RVA's Service Gaps: How 311, Departments, and Partners Can Stop Residents Falling Through the Cracks
summary: Analysis of service gap patterns: requests that fall between 311 categories, services not listed on rva.gov, and residents who cannot find a pathway. Identifies where software can bridge gaps vs. where policy change is required.

title: Richmond's Digital Services: Foundation Built, Recognition Pending
summary: Overview of Richmond's existing digital service infrastructure: online payment portals, permit applications, 311 web and mobile access, and the maturity gap between what exists and what residents know exists.

title: RVA311 (2014–2015) Socrata: What's Usable Now, What's Not
summary: Field-by-field schema analysis of the RVA311 historical Socrata dataset (vgg4-hjn8): 43,273 SeeClickFix records from Jan 2014–Aug 2015. Covers what is usable for prototypes, data quality issues, and why it cannot represent current 311 operations.

title: Richmond City Contracts (xqn7-jvv2) — What's Reliable Now, What to Fix Fast for an Expiry Dashboard
summary: Detailed analysis of the City Contracts Socrata dataset (xqn7-jvv2): field definitions, SODA API access, monthly update cadence, known schema issues (8 vs 9 columns via API), and how to use it for a contract expiry dashboard.

title: Richmond-ready procurement data stack — SAM.gov, USAspending, eVA mapped to a cross-source comparison MVP
summary: Comprehensive mapping of federal and state procurement data sources: SAM.gov API (debarment, entity data), USAspending.gov, and eVA CSV exports. Covers access modes, rate limits, and how to combine for a cross-source comparison tool.

title: Unlocking rva.gov for Service Navigation: Crawl, Structure, and API Reality Check
summary: Technical analysis of rva.gov data accessibility: sitemap.xml coverage, Drupal Search API query patterns, Google Translate widget behavior, and practical approaches for building a content index from public web content.

title: Buildable Prototypes, Honest Data: What's Missing (and What to Do) for RVA's 'Thriving City Hall' Hackathon
summary: Systematic analysis of data quality gaps across all City Hall datasets: missing fields, update lag, access restrictions, and stale content. Provides honest workaround strategies for each gap so teams can build credibly.

title: From Menus to Meaning: Prior Art that Turns 311 Plain Language into the Right City Request
summary: National survey of 311 navigation tools that convert plain-language resident descriptions into routed service requests: Boston's 311 app, Chicago's SeeClickFix, and NLP-assisted triage tools with transferable patterns.

title: From 311 to AI Navigators: What Actually Works in Government Service Discovery
summary: Evidence review of government service navigation tools that have shipped and been adopted: GOV.UK service design patterns, NYC service finder, and AI-assisted navigation pilots. Distinguishes patterns that transfer from those that failed.

title: From Open Data to Decisions: Prior Art Blueprints for a Richmond Contracts Expiry and Vendor Insights Dashboard
summary: Procurement tool prior art with direct application to Richmond: contract expiry dashboards, vendor risk monitoring, and procurement transparency portals from peer cities and federal agencies.

title: Avoiding 'Robodebt-in-Miniature': What Richmond Must Learn from Failed Government Chatbots and Digital Service Tools
summary: Case studies of government digital service failures: Robodebt automation harm, NYC MyCity chatbot hallucinations, chatbot over-confidence, and service abandonment. Extracts design lessons for avoiding the same failures in City Hall tools.

title: Weekend Civic Tech That Works: Fast Paths to Service Navigation and Transparency
summary: Survey of civic tech projects that successfully shipped in hackathon-scale timeframes with public data: what scope, architecture, and data choices enabled success. Directly applicable to 48-hour City Hall builds.

title: Richmond City Hall Hackathon — Fastest Paths to High-Impact, Adoptable Tools
summary: Ranked evaluation of seven solution spaces (A–G) on data readiness, 48-hour feasibility, demo clarity, and adoption potential. Recommends Contract Expiry Dashboard as top build; Plain-Language 311 Helper as second; Service Pathway Mapper as third.

title: Richmond 311 Navigation — Wizard-First, Search-Assisted, Risk-Managed
summary: Detailed opportunity framing for the resident service navigation solution space: wizard-first UX pattern, content index from rva.gov, decision tree logic, and risk management for AI-assisted routing.

title: Richmond's Fastest Path to Procurement Value: A Contract Expiry Dashboard That Wins Champions
summary: Detailed opportunity framing for the procurement solution space: City Contracts Socrata dataset as primary data source, expiry date visualization, filter by department/vendor, and staff champion adoption strategy.

title: Right Tech, Right Place: A practical boundary map for city service automation
summary: Boundary map for where software adds value vs. where it creates harm in City Hall: automation-safe tasks (information retrieval, status display, filtering) vs. human-required tasks (routing decisions, eligibility, complaints with legal implications).

title: Hackathon Red Flags: Six City Hall Builds to Avoid—and What to Ship Instead
summary: Six specific build directions to avoid in the City Hall pillar and safer alternatives for each: live 311 API dependency, eligibility determination tools, procurement automation without human review, and privacy-risky resident data tools.

title: Stopping Confidently Wrong 311 Routing in Richmond: Guardrails for High-Stakes Service Navigation
summary: Risk analysis of incorrect service routing: how wrong routing harms residents, creates duplicate tickets, and erodes trust. Includes guardrail patterns: confidence thresholds, human escalation triggers, and citation requirements.

title: Richmond procurement tools: VPPA risks, failure modes, and safe-by-design controls
summary: Procurement tool risk analysis: Virginia Procurement Act compliance, data freshness failures, hallucinated contract terms, and liability from incorrect contract status displays. Safe-by-design control patterns.

title: Building One City, Not Two: De-risking Richmond's Digital Services for LEP, Low-Broadband, and Accessibility Needs
summary: Inclusion risk analysis for City Hall tools: Limited English Proficiency (LEP) impacts, low-bandwidth access, screen reader compatibility, and design decisions that exclude parts of Richmond's population.

title: Don't Ship Stale: De-risking City Hall Tools from Outdated RVA Data
summary: Data staleness risk analysis: which City Hall datasets have high update lag, how stale service information harms residents, and technical patterns for communicating data freshness to users.

title: City Hall Guardrails That Work: Confidence, Citations, and Human Escalation You Can Ship
summary: Practical guardrail patterns for City Hall AI tools: three-tier confidence UI, inline citations with source chips, 'I'm not sure' escalation, procurement flag routing to human triage, and last-verified date SLAs.

title: 48-Hour City Hall MVPs — What You Can Actually Ship, Safely
summary: 48-hour feasibility framework with specific technical guidance: City Contracts Socrata as primary data source, PyMuPDF over Unstructured for PDF extraction, JSON schema enforcement for LLM routing, and Friday/Saturday/Sunday time budget.

title: RVA Resident Service Navigator MVP: Fastest, Safe Path to a Judge-Ready Demo in 48 Hours
summary: Concrete MVP specification for the resident service navigation build: static decision tree from rva.gov content, wizard UI, 311 call-to-action, and scope limits that prevent unsafe routing claims.

title: Procurement Risk Review MVP: Fastest Credible Path with Lowest Dependencies
summary: Concrete MVP specification for the procurement build: City Contracts Socrata as sole data dependency, expiry date dashboard, department/vendor filters, and credibility features like source citation and last-updated display.

title: No-API, No Problem: Building a Credible RVA311 Navigator
summary: Strategies for building a 311 navigation tool without API access to live 311 data: static content indexing, manual category mapping, rva.gov crawl, and hardcoded decision logic based on documented 311 categories.

title: Buildable-by-Weekend Boundaries for Thriving City Hall MVPs
summary: Explicit scope boundaries for weekend City Hall builds: what must be included for a credible demo, what can be mocked, what must be refused. Provides decision criteria for scope lock.

title: Winning City-Hall Hackathons: Timing-Tight Demo Playbooks that Judges Trust
summary: Four demo archetypes for City Hall solutions with timing guidance, judge expectations, and trust-building elements. Covers what judges look for in civic tech: realistic scope claims, data attribution, and human fallback visibility.

title: Win the Room in 4 Minutes: Richmond-ready 311 Service Navigation Demo Playbook
summary: Step-by-step demo script for the service navigation build: resident persona, problem setup, live wizard walkthrough, guardrail demonstration, and call-to-action. Includes response templates for common judge questions.

title: Winning a Civic Hackathon Demo with a Procurement Tool
summary: Step-by-step demo script for the procurement build: staff persona, contract expiry scenario, dashboard walkthrough, data sourcing disclosure, and adoption narrative for judge credibility.

title: Credible City Hall Demos: Proving Real Data, Real Limits, Real Trust
summary: Credibility elements that separate winning City Hall demos from hallucination-risk pitches: explicit data source citations on screen, visible scope limits, human fallback flows, and honest uncertainty language.

title: This Helps X Do Y: City Hall Demo Framing That Wins Trust
summary: Framing strategies for City Hall demos: user-first narrative structure, specific problem-solution anchoring, avoiding over-engineered AI claims, and language patterns that resonate with civic judges and city staff.
